perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information . As used herein, “ Confidential Information ” shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information previously, presently, or subsequently disclosed or provided by Disclosing Party and/or one or more of its Affiliates to Receiving Party and/or one or more of its Affiliates in written, oral or electronic form. Confidential Information will be deemed to include, without limitation: (a) any technology, inventions, products, chemical compounds and compositions, formulations, molecules, precursors, methods, concepts, ideas, plans, processes, specifications, characteristics, techniques, know-how and assays; clinical information such as raw data, scientific preclinical or clinical data, observations, records, databases, dosing regimes, clinical studies or protocols, posters, presentations and abstracts, product pipelines, timelines and schedules; business information such as development, marketing, sales, pricing and commercialization plans, forecasts, proposals, customer lists, suppliers, consulting relationships, operating, performance and cost structures, and any other non-public information or other trade secrets, whether scientific, clinical or financial in nature, relating directly or indirectly to the business of the Disclosing Party; and (b) any memorandum, analysis, compilation, summary, interpretation, study, report or other document, record or material that is or has been prepared by or for the Receiving Party and that contains, reflects, interprets or is based directly or indirectly upon any information of the type referred to in Section 2(a) above; (c) the existence and terms of this Agreement, and the fact that information of the type referred to in Section 2(a) above has been made available to the Receiving Party; and (d) the fact that discussions or negotiations are or may be taking place with respect to a possible transaction involving the Parties, and the proposed terms of any such transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information . As used herein, “ Confidential Information ” shall mean only written technical information clearly marked as confidential at the time of disclosure. Confidential Information will be deemed to include, without limitation: (a) any technology, inventions, products, chemical compounds and compositions, formulations, molecules, precursors, methods, concepts, ideas, plans, processes, specifications, characteristics, techniques, know-how and assays; clinical information such as raw data, scientific preclinical or clinical data, observations, records, databases, dosing regimes, clinical studies or protocols, posters, presentations and abstracts, product pipelines, timelines and schedules; business information such as development, marketing, sales, pricing and commercialization plans, forecasts, proposals, customer lists, suppliers, consulting relationships, operating, performance and cost structures, and any other non-public information or other trade secrets, whether scientific, clinical or financial in nature, relating directly or indirectly to the business of the Disclosing Party; and (b) any memorandum, analysis, compilation, summary, interpretation, study, report or other document, record or material that is or has been prepared by or for the Receiving Party and that contains, reflects, interprets or is based directly or indirectly upon any information of the type referred to in Section 2(a) above; (c) the existence and terms of this Agreement, and the fact that information of the type referred to in Section 2(a) above has been made available to the Receiving Party; and (d) the fact that discussions or negotiations are or may be taking place with respect to a possible transaction involving the Parties, and the proposed terms of any such transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The original text broadly defines 'Confidential Information' to include all technical and non-technical information in any form. The changed text limits this definition to only written technical information explicitly marked as confidential. This creates a contradiction as it narrows the scope of what is considered confidential, conflicting with the previously broader definition. This change directly contradicts the original broad scope. Specifically the inclusion of 'non-technical information' which is now no longer under the definition of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information . As used herein, “ Confidential Information ” shall mean only written technical information clearly marked as confidential at the time of disclosure. Confidential Information will be deemed to include, without limitation: (a) any technology, inventions, products, chemical compounds and compositions, formulations, molecules, precursors, methods, concepts, ideas, plans, processes, specifications, characteristics, techniques, know-how and assays; clinical information such as raw data, scientific preclinical or clinical data, observations, records, databases, dosing regimes, clinical studies or protocols, posters, presentations and abstracts, product pipelines, timelines and schedules; business information such as development, marketing, sales, pricing and commercialization plans, forecasts, proposals, customer lists, suppliers, consulting relationships, operating, performance and cost structures, and any other non-public information or other trade secrets, whether scientific, clinical or financial in nature, relating directly or indirectly to the business of the Disclosing Party; and (b) any memorandum, analysis, compilation, summary, interpretation, study, report or other document, record or material that is or has been prepared by or for the Receiving Party and that contains, reflects, interprets or is based directly or indirectly upon any information of the type referred to in Section 2(a) above; (c) the existence and terms of this Agreement, and the fact that information of the type referred to in Section 2(a) above has been made available to the Receiving Party; and (d) the fact that discussions or negotiations are or may be taking place with respect to a possible transaction involving the Parties, and the proposed terms of any such transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, encompassing all technical and non-technical information. The changed text narrows this definition to only 'written technical information clearly marked as confidential.' This directly contradicts the original definition by excluding non-technical and unmarked information, creating a strong in-text contradiction with the continued use of the original broader definition in subsequent sections of the document.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose defined above. Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose, including but not limited to using it in connection with the development or commercialization of any process or product, or using it in connection with any submission to any governmental agency, including any patent office or regulatory authority, or the like, without the express written permission of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose defined above, except that Receiving Party may use the Confidential Information to improve its own business processes. Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose, including but not limited to using it in connection with the development or commercialization of any process or product, or using it in connection with any submission to any governmental agency, including any patent office or regulatory authority, or the like, without the express written permission of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits the Receiving Party from using the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the defined 'Purpose'. The changed text adds an exception, allowing the Receiving Party to use the information to improve its own business processes. This directly contradicts the previous restriction on use for 'any other purpose,' creating ambiguity about the extent of permissible use and it is in direct contradiction with that statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Treatment of Confidential Information . (a) Use; Disclosure . Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose defined above, except that Receiving Party may use the Confidential Information to improve its own business processes. Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose, including but not limited to using it in connection with the development or commercialization of any process or product, or using it in connection with any submission to any governmental agency, including any patent office or regulatory authority, or the like, without the express written permission of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original strict prohibition on using confidential information for purposes other than the defined 'Purpose'. The addition of 'except that Receiving Party may use the Confidential Information to improve its own business processes' directly contradicts the subsequent statement that the Receiving Party 'shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the modified text both allows and prohibits the use of confidential information for purposes beyond the explicitly defined 'Purpose', creating ambiguity and potential unenforceability. Additionally, the Contradicted Text section is the same as the Changed Text, further proving the contradiction is within that section of the document itself.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party will promptly turn over to Disclosing Party, or destroy, all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party and all documents, media, and other tangible materials containing any such Confidential Information and any and all extracts thereof. In the event that Receiving Party destroys Confidential Information, upon the destruction thereof, Receiving Party will issue to Disclosing Party a certificate as proof of compliance with Disclosing Party’s request. Notwithstanding this Section 4(f) , the Receiving Party shall not be required to purge Confidential Information from its computer system’s historical back-up media, provided that such Confidential Information that is retained will remain subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party will promptly turn over to Disclosing Party, or destroy, all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party and all documents, media, and other tangible materials containing any such Confidential Information and any and all extracts thereof. In the event that Receiving Party destroys Confidential Information, upon the destruction thereof, Receiving Party will issue to Disclosing Party a certificate as proof of compliance with Disclosing Party’s request. Notwithstanding this Section 4(f) , the Receiving Party shall be required to purge Confidential Information from its computer system’s historical back-up media, regardless if such Confidential Information that is retained will remain subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Receiving Party is not required to purge Confidential Information from its computer systems' historical back-up media. The modified text mandates that the Receiving Party must purge Confidential Information, directly contradicting the original exception. Purging and not purging are in direct opposition and cause uncertainty regarding data management.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials . Immediately upon (a) termination or expiration of this Agreement, (b) the decision by either Party not to enter into the business or scientific relationship contemplated above, or (c) a request by Disclosing Party at any time, Receiving Party will promptly turn over to Disclosing Party, or destroy, all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party and all documents, media, and other tangible materials containing any such Confidential Information and any and all extracts thereof. In the event that Receiving Party destroys Confidential Information, upon the destruction thereof, Receiving Party will issue to Disclosing Party a certificate as proof of compliance with Disclosing Party’s request. Notwithstanding this Section 4(f) , the Receiving Party shall be required to purge Confidential Information from its computer system’s historical back-up media, regardless if such Confidential Information that is retained will remain subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Receiving Party is not required to purge Confidential Information from its computer system’s historical back-up media. The changed text mandates that the Receiving Party must purge Confidential Information, directly contradicting the original exception. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, reinforcing the presence of the contradiction as the modified section now conflicts with the original intent of not requiring purging under certain circumstances.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties do not intend that any agency or partnership relationship be created between them by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties do not intend that any agency or partnership relationship be created between them by this Agreement, but in practice, an agency relationship may exist.
perturbation.explanation: The original states that no agency or partnership is intended. The addition suggests that, despite intentions, one may arise in practice, contradicting the former assertion and introducing ambiguity. The phrases 'do not intend' and 'may exist' create direct opposition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Independence . The Parties do not intend that any agency or partnership relationship be created between them by this Agreement, but in practice, an agency relationship may exist.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no agency or partnership relationship is intended. The changed text adds 'but in practice, an agency relationship may exist'. This directly contradicts the original intention by suggesting an agency relationship could still arise, creating ambiguity and potential unenforceability. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which shows that the change introduces a self-contradiction. The phrase 'do not intend' clashes with 'may exist', and falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction: where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, leading to uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.
